titlStmt.titl: Qualitative Longitudinal Research (QLR) on integration after Amenity Migration in two Alpine case study regions in Western Austria between 2015 and 2021 (OA edition)

citation.biblCit: Grüner, Bernhard; Konzett, Savina, 2021, "Qualitative Longitudinal Research (QLR) on integration after Amenity Migration in two Alpine case study regions in Western Austria between 2015 and 2021 (OA edition)", https://doi.org/10.11587/MWSGOS, AUSSDA, V2

titlStmt.titl: Qualitative Longitudinal Research (QLR) on integration after Amenity Migration in two Alpine case study regions in Western Austria between 2015 and 2021 (OA edition)

distStmt.distrbtr: The Austrian Social Science Data Archive

contact.affiliation: The Austrian Social Science Data Archive

stdyInfo.abstract: Full edition for public use. The dataset provides insights into the integration process after a permanent or temporary lifestyle-related relocation to remote Alpine regions of Austria. It highlights factors which are decisive for staying or relocating again, and how staying affects space and society. This Qualitative Longitudinal Research (QLR) is based on a cross-sectional study from 2015/2016, which was extended to a catch-up study in 2020/2021. The analysis of a total of 53 semi-structured as well as problem-centred interviews with communal experts and newcomers in the Vorarlberg Montafon and Tiroler Gailtal was supported by the Grounded Theory. The dataset primarily contains a code table with a total of 272 codes and subcodes on migration triggers and the integration process of newcomers, but due to research ethics do not include related interview transcripts or direct quotes. Additional information is also added to the data set to provide a better understanding of the results and data: i.e. illustrations, interview guidelines, the current data management plan, an informed consent form and the metadata of interviews and illustrations.

sumDscr.universe: The object of study are people who have relocated their residence permanently or temporarily to the case study communities of Gaschurn and Bartholomäberg (Montafon in Vorarlberg) as well as Obertilliach and Untertilliach (Tyrolean Gail Valley) due to amenities respective lifestyle reasons. These amenities include natural and cultural characteristics (landscape, climate, remoteness, etc.) as well as advantages for personal mental and physical health (sports and leisure activities, etc.). The sample was defined by the snowball principle, with the aim of theoretical saturation according to the Grounded Theory.

titlStmt.titl: Grüner, B., & Konzett, S. (2021). Come to Stay? Integration After Amenity Migration to the Alps - Qualitative Longitudinal Research in two Peripheral Mountain Regions of Western Austria. Vienna, Annals of the Austrian Geographical Society, 163, 199–235.

citation.biblCit: Grüner, B., & Konzett, S. (2021). Come to Stay? Integration After Amenity Migration to the Alps - Qualitative Longitudinal Research in two Peripheral Mountain Regions of Western Austria. Vienna, Annals of the Austrian Geographical Society, 163, 199–235.

titlStmt.titl: Grüner, B., & Konzett, S. (2017). Gegen den Strom - Neue Zuwanderung in den alpinen Westen Österreichs. Innsbruck, Institut für Geographie der Universität Innsbruck in Zusammenarbeit mit der Innsbrucker Geographischen Gesellschaft: Innsbrucker Jahresbericht 2016–2017, 302.

citation.biblCit: Grüner, B., & Konzett, S. (2017). Gegen den Strom - Neue Zuwanderung in den alpinen Westen Österreichs. Innsbruck, Institut für Geographie der Universität Innsbruck in Zusammenarbeit mit der Innsbrucker Geographischen Gesellschaft: Innsbrucker Jahresbericht 2016–2017, 302.
